TRIO Device API (gxio/trio.h)
[gxio Low-Level IO Device Control API]

Detailed Description

The TILE-Gx TRIO shim provides connections to external devices via PCIe or other transaction IO standards.

The gxio_trio_ API, declared in <gxio/trio.h>, allows applications to allocate and configure TRIO IO resources like DMA command rings, memory map windows, and device interrupts. The following sections introduce the various components of the API. We strongly recommend reading the TRIO section of the IO Device Guide (UG404) before working with this API.

TRIO Ingress Hardware Resources

The TRIO ingress hardware is responsible for examining incoming PCIe or StreamIO packets and choosing a processing mechanism based on the packets' bus address. The gxio_trio_ API can be used to configure different handlers for different ranges of bus address space. The user can configure "mapped memory" and "scatter queue" regions to match incoming packets within 4kB-aligned ranges of bus addresses. Each range specifies a different set of mapping parameters to be applied when handling the ingress packet. The following sections describe how to work with MapMem and scatter queue regions.

TRIO MapMem Regions

TRIO mapped memory (or MapMem) regions allow the user to map incoming read and write requests directly to the application's memory space. MapMem regions are allocated via gxio_trio_alloc_memory_maps(). Given an integer MapMem number, applications can use gxio_trio_init_memory_map() to specify the range of bus addresses that will match the region and the range of virtual addresses to which those packets will be applied.

As with many other gxio APIs, the programmer must be sure to register memory pages that will be used with MapMem regions. Pages can be registered with TRIO by allocating an ASID (address space identifier) and then using gxio_trio_register_page() to register up to 16 pages with the hardware. The initialization functions for resources that require registered memory (MapMem, scatter queues, push DMA, and pull DMA) then take an 'asid' parameter in order to configure which set of registered pages is used by each resource.

TRIO Scatter Queues

The TRIO shim's scatter queue regions allow users to dynamically map buffers from a large address space into a small range of bus addresses. This is particularly helpful for PCIe endpoint devices, where the host generally limits the size of BARs to tens of megabytes.

Each scatter queue consists of a memory map region, a queue of tile-side buffer VAs to be mapped to that region, and a bus-mapped "doorbell" register that the remote endpoint can write to trigger a dequeue of the current buffer VA, thus swapping in a new buffer. The VAs pushed onto a scatter queue must be 4kB aligned, so applications may need to use higher-level protocols to inform remote entities that they should apply some additional, sub-4kB offset when reading or writing the scatter queue region. For more information, see the IO Device Guide (UG404).

TRIO Egress Hardware Resources

The TRIO shim supports two mechanisms for egress packet generation: programmed IO (PIO) and push/pull DMA. PIO allows applications to create MMIO mappings for PCIe or StreamIO address space, such that the application can generate word-sized read or write transactions by issuing load or store instructions. Push and pull DMA are tuned for larger transactions; they use specialized hardware engines to transfer large blocks of data at line rate.

TRIO Programmed IO

Programmed IO allows applications to create MMIO mappings for PCIe or StreamIO address space. The hardware PIO regions support access to PCIe configuration, IO, and memory space, but the gxio_trio API only supports memory space accesses. PIO regions are allocated with gxio_trio_alloc_pio_regions() and initialized via gxio_trio_init_pio_region(). Once a region is bound to a range of bus address via the initialization function, the application can use gxio_trio_map_pio_region() to create MMIO mappings from its VA space onto the range of bus addresses supported by the PIO region.

TRIO Push and Pull DMA

The TRIO push and pull DMA engines allow users to copy blocks of data between application memory and the bus. Push DMA generates write packets that copy from application memory to the bus and pull DMA generates read packets that copy from the bus into application memory. The DMA engines are managed via an API that is very similar to the mPIPE eDMA interface. For a detailed explanation of the eDMA queue API, see mPIPE Ingress and Egress Wrappers.

Push and pull DMA queues are allocated via gxio_trio_alloc_push_dma_ring() / gxio_trio_alloc_pull_dma_ring(). Once allocated, users generally use a gxio_trio_dma_queue_t object to manage the queue, providing easy wrappers for reserving command slots in the DMA command ring, filling those slots, and waiting for commands to complete. DMA queues can be initialized via gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue() or gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue().

See trio/push_dma/appc for an example of how to use push DMA.

Plans for Future API Revisions

The simulation framework is incomplete. Future features include:

  • Support for reset and deallocation of resources.

  • Support for pull DMA.

  • Support for interrupt regions and user-space interrupt delivery.

  • Support for getting BAR mappings and reserving regions of BAR address space.

Data Structures

union  TRIO_DMA_DESC_t
 TRIO DMA Descriptor. More...
struct  gxio_trio_context_t
 A context object used to manage TRIO hardware resources. More...
struct  gxio_trio_dma_queue_t
 A convenient, thread-safe interface to an eDMA ring. More...

Defines

#define GXIO_ASID_NULL   -1
 This indicates that an ASID hasn't been allocated.
#define GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_TRAFFIC_CLASS(N)   HV_TRIO_FLAG_TC(N)
 Flag indicating a request generator uses a special traffic class.
#define GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_VFUNC(N)   HV_TRIO_FLAG_VFUNC(N)
 Flag indicating a request generator uses a virtual function number.
#define GXIO_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_ORDERED   HV_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_ORDERED
 Flag indicating a strictly ordered PIO region.
#define GXIO_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_CONFIG_SPACE   HV_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_CONFIG_SPACE
 Flag indicating a config space PIO region.
#define GXIO_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_IO_SPACE   HV_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_IO_SPACE
 Flag indicating an IO space PIO region.

Typedefs

typedef TRIO_DMA_DESC_t gxio_trio_dma_desc_t
 Command descriptor for push or pull DMA.
typedef enum gxio_trio_order_mode_e gxio_trio_order_mode_t
 Ordering modes for map memory regions and scatter queue regions.

Enumerations

enum  gxio_trio_order_mode_e { GXIO_TRIO_ORDER_MODE_UNORDERED, GXIO_TRIO_ORDER_MODE_STRICT, GXIO_TRIO_ORDER_MODE_OBEY_PACKET }
 Ordering modes for map memory regions and scatter queue regions. More...
enum  gxio_trio_alloc_flags_e { GXIO_TRIO_ALLOC_FIXED = HV_TRIO_ALLOC_FIXED }
 Flags that can be passed to resource allocation functions. More...
enum  gxio_trio_mem_flags_e { GXIO_TRIO_MEM_FLAG_NT_HINT = IORPC_MEM_BUFFER_FLAG_NT_HINT, GXIO_TRIO_MEM_FLAG_IO_PIN = IORPC_MEM_BUFFER_FLAG_IO_PIN }
 Flags that can be passed to memory registration functions. More...
enum  gxio_trio_mmi_write_effect_t { GXIO_TRIO_MMI_BITS_WRITE = 0, GXIO_TRIO_MMI_BITS_W1TC = 1, GXIO_TRIO_MMI_BITS_W1TS = 2 }
 Actions that can occur when memory map interrupt bits are written via Tile-side MMIO. More...
enum  gxio_trio_mmi_mode_t { GXIO_TRIO_MMI_MODE_LEVEL = TRIO_MAP_MEM_SETUP__INT_MODE_VAL_LEVEL, GXIO_TRIO_MMI_MODE_EDGE = TRIO_MAP_MEM_SETUP__INT_MODE_VAL_EDGE, GXIO_TRIO_MMI_MODE_ASSERT = TRIO_MAP_MEM_SETUP__INT_MODE_VAL_ASSERT, GXIO_TRIO_MMI_MODE_DEASSERT = TRIO_MAP_MEM_SETUP__INT_MODE_VAL_DEASSERT }
 Interrupt trigger modes for bus-to-tile interrupts. More...

Functions

int gxio_trio_destroy (gxio_trio_context_t *context)
 Destroy a TRIO context.
int gxio_trio_init (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int trio_index)
 Initialize a TRIO context.
int gxio_trio_free_memory_map (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int map)
 Free a memory mapping region.
int gxio_trio_init_memory_map (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int map, void *target_mem, size_t target_size, unsigned int asid, unsigned int mac, uint64_t bus_address, gxio_trio_order_mode_t order_mode)
 Initialize a memory mapping region.
int gxio_trio_dealloc_asid (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int asid)
 De-allocate an allocated Application Space Identifiers (ASIDs).
int gxio_trio_alloc_asids (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int count, unsigned int first, unsigned int flags)
 Allocate Application Space Identifiers (ASIDs).
int gxio_trio_unregister_page (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int asid, void *page)
 Unregister a page with an ASID.
int gxio_trio_register_page (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int asid, void *page, size_t page_size, unsigned int page_flags)
 Register a page with an ASID.
int gxio_trio_free_push_dma_ring (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int ring)
 Free a push DMA ring.
int gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue (gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *queue, gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int ring, unsigned int mac, unsigned int asid, unsigned int req_flags, void *mem, unsigned int mem_size, unsigned int mem_flags)
 Initialize a gxio_trio_dma_queue_t for use with push DMA.
int gxio_trio_free_pull_dma_ring (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int ring)
 Free a pull DMA ring.
int gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue (gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *queue, gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int ring, unsigned int mac, unsigned int asid, unsigned int req_flags, void *mem, unsigned int mem_size, unsigned int mem_flags)
 Initialize a gxio_trio_dma_queue_t for use with pull DMA.
int gxio_trio_free_scatter_queue (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int queue)
 Free a scatter queue.
int64_t gxio_trio_dma_queue_reserve (gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *queue, unsigned int num)
 Reserve slots for DMA commands.
void gxio_trio_dma_queue_put_at (gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *queue, gxio_trio_dma_desc_t dma_desc, uint64_t slot)
 Post a DMA command to a DMA queue at a given slot.
void gxio_trio_dma_queue_flush (gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *queue)
 Ask the TRIO hardware to start DMA immediately.
uint16_t gxio_trio_read_dma_queue_complete_count (gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *queue)
 Find out how many DMA queue descriptors have been completely processed.
int gxio_trio_read_isr_status (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int vec_num)
 Read out the interrupt status from TRIO_INT_VEC0 to 4.
int gxio_trio_write_isr_status (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int vec_num, uint32_t bits_to_clear)
 Write the interrupt status to TRIO_INT_VEC0 to 4 to clear particular interrupts.
int gxio_trio_trigger_host_interrupt (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int mac, unsigned int can_spin, unsigned long msix_addr, unsigned int msix_data)
 Trigger the PCIe RC host MSI/MSI-X interrupt.
int gxio_trio_trigger_host_interrupt_vf (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int mac, unsigned int can_spin, unsigned long msix_addr, unsigned int msix_data, unsigned int vf)
 Trigger the PCIe RC host MSI/MSI-X interrupt for a virtual function.
int gxio_trio_write_mmi_bits (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int map, unsigned int bits, gxio_trio_mmi_write_effect_t mode)
 Write the bus-to-tile interrupt state bits associated with a memory map region.
int gxio_trio_alloc_memory_maps (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int count, unsigned int first, unsigned int flags)
 Allocate a memory map region for mapping incoming bus transactions to Tile memory loads and stores.
int gxio_trio_enable_mmi (gxio_trio_context_t *context, int bind_cpu, int bind_event, unsigned int map, gxio_trio_mmi_mode_t mode)
 Enable bus-to-tile interrupts for a particular memory map region.
int gxio_trio_mask_mmi (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int map, unsigned int mask)
 Prevent a bus-to-tile memory map interrupt from being delivered.
int gxio_trio_unmask_mmi (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int map, unsigned int mask)
 Allow a bus-to-tile memory map interrupt to be delivered.
int gxio_trio_read_mmi_bits (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int map)
 Read the bus-to-tile interrupt state bits associated with a memory map region.
int gxio_trio_alloc_scatter_queues (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int count, unsigned int first, unsigned int flags)
 Allocate scatter queues.
int gxio_trio_init_scatter_queue (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int queue, uint64_t size, unsigned int asid, unsigned int mac, uint64_t bus_address, gxio_trio_order_mode_t order_mode)

void gxio_trio_push_scatter_queue_buffer (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int queue, void *buffer, int request_interrupt)
 Push a buffer onto a scatter queue.
uint8_t gxio_trio_read_scatter_queue_pop_count (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int queue)
 Find out how many scatter queue VAs have been dequeued.
int gxio_trio_enable_sqi (gxio_trio_context_t *context, int bind_cpu, int bind_event, unsigned int queue)
 Enable bus-to-tile interrupts for a particular scatter queue.
int gxio_trio_alloc_pio_regions (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int count, unsigned int first, unsigned int flags)
 Allocate programmed I/O (PIO) regions.
int gxio_trio_free_pio_region (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int pio_region)
 Free a PIO region.
int gxio_trio_init_pio_region (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int pio_region, unsigned int mac, uint64_t bus_address, unsigned int flags)
 Initialize a PIO region.
int gxio_trio_unmap_pio_region (void *pio_mmio_addr, unsigned int length)
 Unmap a PIO region from VA space.
void * gxio_trio_map_pio_region (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int pio_region, unsigned int length, unsigned int offset)
 Map some or all of a PIO region into VA space.
void gxio_trio_write_uint64 (void *addr, uint64_t val)
 Store eight bytes to a PIO mapping.
void gxio_trio_write_uint32 (void *addr, uint32_t val)
 Store four bytes to a PIO mapping.
void gxio_trio_write_uint16 (void *addr, uint16_t val)
 Store two bytes to a PIO mapping.
void gxio_trio_write_uint8 (void *addr, uint8_t val)
 Store a byte to a PIO mapping.
uint64_t gxio_trio_read_uint64 (void *addr)
 Load eight bytes from a PIO mapping.
uint32_t gxio_trio_read_uint32 (void *addr)
 Load four bytes from a PIO mapping.
uint16_t gxio_trio_read_uint16 (void *addr)
 Load two bytes from a PIO mapping.
uint8_t gxio_trio_read_uint8 (void *addr)
 Load a byte from a PIO mapping.
int gxio_trio_alloc_push_dma_ring (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int count, unsigned int first, unsigned int flags)
 Allocate push DMA rings.
int gxio_trio_alloc_pull_dma_ring (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int count, unsigned int first, unsigned int flags)
 Allocate pull DMA rings.
int gxio_trio_init_push_dma_ring (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int ring, unsigned int mac, unsigned int asid, unsigned int req_flags, void *mem, size_t mem_size, unsigned int mem_flags)
 Initialize a push DMA ring.
int gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_ring (gxio_trio_context_t *context, unsigned int ring, unsigned int mac, unsigned int asid, unsigned int req_flags, void *mem, size_t mem_size, unsigned int mem_flags)
 Initialize a pull DMA ring.
int gxio_trio_enable_push_dma_isr (gxio_trio_context_t *context, int bind_cpu, int bind_event, unsigned int ring)
 Enable push dma completion interrupt for a particular push dma ring.
int gxio_trio_enable_pull_dma_isr (gxio_trio_context_t *context, int bind_cpu, int bind_event, unsigned int ring)
 Enable pull dma completion interrupt for a particular pull dma ring.
int64_t gxio_trio_dma_queue_try_reserve (gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *queue, unsigned int num)
 Reserve slots for DMA commands, if possible.
int gxio_trio_dma_queue_put (gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *queue, gxio_trio_dma_desc_t dma_desc)
 Post a single DMA command to an DMA queue.
bool gxio_trio_dma_queue_is_complete (gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *queue, int64_t slot, bool update)
 Determine if a given DMA command has been completed.

Define Documentation

#define GXIO_ASID_NULL   -1

This indicates that an ASID hasn't been allocated.

Examples:
trio/gxpci_c2c/c2c_send.c, and trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c.

#define GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_TRAFFIC_CLASS ( N   )     HV_TRIO_FLAG_TC(N)

Flag indicating a request generator uses a special traffic class.

#define GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_VFUNC ( N   )     HV_TRIO_FLAG_VFUNC(N)

Flag indicating a request generator uses a virtual function number.

#define GXIO_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_ORDERED   HV_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_ORDERED

Flag indicating a strictly ordered PIO region.

#define GXIO_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_CONFIG_SPACE   HV_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_CONFIG_SPACE

Flag indicating a config space PIO region.

#define GXIO_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_IO_SPACE   HV_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_IO_SPACE

Flag indicating an IO space PIO region.

Typedef Documentation

typedef TRIO_DMA_DESC_t gxio_trio_dma_desc_t

Command descriptor for push or pull DMA.

typedef enum gxio_trio_order_mode_e gxio_trio_order_mode_t

Ordering modes for map memory regions and scatter queue regions.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum gxio_trio_order_mode_e

Ordering modes for map memory regions and scatter queue regions.

Enumerator:
GXIO_TRIO_ORDER_MODE_UNORDERED  Writes are not ordered.

Reads always wait for previous writes.

GXIO_TRIO_ORDER_MODE_STRICT  Both writes and reads wait for previous transactions to complete.

GXIO_TRIO_ORDER_MODE_OBEY_PACKET  Writes are ordered unless the incoming packet has the relaxed-ordering attributes set.

enum gxio_trio_alloc_flags_e

Flags that can be passed to resource allocation functions.

enum gxio_trio_mem_flags_e

Flags that can be passed to memory registration functions.

Enumerator:
GXIO_TRIO_MEM_FLAG_NT_HINT  Do not fill L3 when writing, and invalidate lines upon egress.

GXIO_TRIO_MEM_FLAG_IO_PIN  L3 cache fills should only populate IO cache ways.

enum gxio_trio_mmi_write_effect_t

Actions that can occur when memory map interrupt bits are written via Tile-side MMIO.

enum gxio_trio_mmi_mode_t

Interrupt trigger modes for bus-to-tile interrupts.

Enumerator:
GXIO_TRIO_MMI_MODE_LEVEL  Interrupt fires continuously if any interrupt bits are 1.

GXIO_TRIO_MMI_MODE_EDGE  Interrupt fires on a rising or falling edge.

GXIO_TRIO_MMI_MODE_ASSERT  Interrupt fires on a rising edge.

GXIO_TRIO_MMI_MODE_DEASSERT  Interrupt fires on a falling edge.

Function Documentation

int gxio_trio_destroy ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context  ) 

Destroy a TRIO context.

This function de-allocates a TRIO "service domain" and unmaps the MMIO registers from the the caller's VA space.

Parameters:
context An initialized context object to be destroyed, returned by gxio_trio_init().

int gxio_trio_init ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  trio_index 
)

Initialize a TRIO context.

This function allocates a TRIO "service domain" and maps the MMIO registers into the the caller's VA space.

Parameters:
context Context object to be initialized.
trio_index Which TRIO shim; Gx36 must pass 0.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_c2c/c2c_send.c, trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c, trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c, trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and trio/push_dma/app.c.

int gxio_trio_free_memory_map ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  map 
)

Free a memory mapping region.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
map An initialized Memory map region.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_MEMORY_MAP if illegal memory map region.

int gxio_trio_init_memory_map ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  map,
void *  target_mem,
size_t  target_size,
unsigned int  asid,
unsigned int  mac,
uint64_t  bus_address,
gxio_trio_order_mode_t  order_mode 
)

Initialize a memory mapping region.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
map A Memory map region allocated by gxio_trio_alloc_memory_maps().
target_mem VA of backing memory, should be registered via gxio_trio_register_page() and aligned to 4kB.
target_size Length of the memory mapping, must be a multiple of 4kB.
asid ASID to be used for Tile-side address translation.
mac MAC number.
bus_address Bus address at which the mapping starts.
order_mode Memory ordering mode for this mapping.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_MEMORY_MAP, GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_ASID, or GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_BUS_RANGE.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and trio/push_dma/app.c.

int gxio_trio_dealloc_asid ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  asid 
)

De-allocate an allocated Application Space Identifiers (ASIDs).

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
asid Index of ASID to be de-allocated.
Returns:
0 on success, or GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_ASID if de-allocation failed.

int gxio_trio_alloc_asids ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  count,
unsigned int  first,
unsigned int  flags 
)

Allocate Application Space Identifiers (ASIDs).

Each ASID can register up to 16 page translations. ASIDs are used by memory map regions, scatter queues, and DMA queues to translate application VAs into memory system PAs.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
count Number of ASIDs required.
first Index of first ASID if GXIO_TRIO_ALLOC_FIXED flag is set, otherwise ignored.
flags Flag bits, including bits from gxio_trio_alloc_flags_e.
Returns:
Index of first ASID, or GXIO_TRIO_ERR_NO_ASID if allocation failed.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c, trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and trio/push_dma/app.c.

int gxio_trio_unregister_page ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  asid,
void *  page 
)

Unregister a page with an ASID.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
asid An ASID allocated via gxio_trio_alloc_asids().
page Starting VA of a contiguous memory page.
Returns:
Zero on success, EINVAL if page does not map a contiguous page, GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_ASID if illegal ASID or GXIO_ERR_IOTLB_ENTRY if no registered IOTLB entry is found.

int gxio_trio_register_page ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  asid,
void *  page,
size_t  page_size,
unsigned int  page_flags 
)

Register a page with an ASID.

Each ASID can map up to 16 pages. Tile-side memory addresses generated by memory map regions, scatter queues, push DMA rings, and pull DMA rings must reference a VA that has been registered with this function.

Note that the underlying driver internally marks the registered page shared, as if it is allocated with mmap(2) flag MAP_SHARED, which implies that the memory is shared between the allocating process and any child processes created after the allocation.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
asid An ASID allocated via gxio_trio_alloc_asids().
page Starting VA of a contiguous memory page.
page_size Size of the page.
page_flags gxio_trio_mem_flags_e memory flags.
Returns:
Zero on success, EINVAL if page does not map a contiguous page, GXIO_ERR_IOTLB_ENTRY if no more IOTLB entries are available.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c, trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and trio/push_dma/app.c.

int gxio_trio_free_push_dma_ring ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  ring 
)

Free a push DMA ring.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
ring An initialized push DMA ring.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_PUSH_DMA_RING if illegal push DMA ring.

int gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue ( gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *  queue,
gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  ring,
unsigned int  mac,
unsigned int  asid,
unsigned int  req_flags,
void *  mem,
unsigned int  mem_size,
unsigned int  mem_flags 
)

Initialize a gxio_trio_dma_queue_t for use with push DMA.

Takes the queue plus the same args as gxio_trio_init_push_dma_ring().

Examples:
trio/push_dma/app.c.

int gxio_trio_free_pull_dma_ring ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  ring 
)

Free a pull DMA ring.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
ring An initialized pull DMA ring.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_PULL_DMA_RING if illegal pull DMA ring.

int gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue ( gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *  queue,
gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  ring,
unsigned int  mac,
unsigned int  asid,
unsigned int  req_flags,
void *  mem,
unsigned int  mem_size,
unsigned int  mem_flags 
)

Initialize a gxio_trio_dma_queue_t for use with pull DMA.

Takes the queue plus the same args as gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_ring().

int gxio_trio_free_scatter_queue ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue 
)

Free a scatter queue.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
queue An initialized scatter queue.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_SCATTER_QUEUE if illegal scatter queue region.

int64_t gxio_trio_dma_queue_reserve ( gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *  queue,
unsigned int  num 
)

Reserve slots for DMA commands.

Use gxio_trio_dma_queue_put_at() to actually populate the slots.

Parameters:
queue An dma queue initialized via gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue() or gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue().
num Number of slots to reserve.
Returns:
The first reserved slot, or a negative error code.
Examples:
trio/push_dma/app.c.

void gxio_trio_dma_queue_put_at ( gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *  queue,
gxio_trio_dma_desc_t  dma_desc,
uint64_t  slot 
)

Post a DMA command to a DMA queue at a given slot.

This routine is often used to insert DMA commands into the queue in a pre-determined order. The implementation does not check to make sure DMA command credits are available. It is assumed that the user knows the ring can never overflow, generally because the application uses fewer buffers than there are slots in the DMA queue.

Parameters:
queue An dma queue initialized via gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue() or gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue().
dma_desc DMA command to be posted.
slot An egress slot (only the low bits are actually used).
Examples:
trio/push_dma/app.c.

void gxio_trio_dma_queue_flush ( gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *  queue  ) 

Ask the TRIO hardware to start DMA immediately.

This call is not necessary, but may slightly reduce overall latency.

Parameters:
queue A DMA queue initialized via gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue() or gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue().

uint16_t gxio_trio_read_dma_queue_complete_count ( gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *  queue  ) 

Find out how many DMA queue descriptors have been completely processed.

Parameters:
queue A DMA queue initialized via gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue() or gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue().
Returns:
A 16-bit running count of the number of descriptors that have been completely processed.

int gxio_trio_read_isr_status ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  vec_num 
)

Read out the interrupt status from TRIO_INT_VEC0 to 4.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
vec_num Interrupt vector number.
Returns:
Interrupt status on success, else GXIO_ERR_INVAL.

int gxio_trio_write_isr_status ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  vec_num,
uint32_t  bits_to_clear 
)

Write the interrupt status to TRIO_INT_VEC0 to 4 to clear particular interrupts.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
vec_num Interrupt vector number.
bits_to_clear Interrupt bits to be cleared within the paticular interrupt vector.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_ERR_INVAL.

int gxio_trio_trigger_host_interrupt ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  mac,
unsigned int  can_spin,
unsigned long  msix_addr,
unsigned int  msix_data 
)

Trigger the PCIe RC host MSI/MSI-X interrupt.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
mac MAC number.
can_spin Flag indicating if this can spin.
msix_addr MSI-X message address.
msix_data MSI-X message data.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_ERR_BUSY.

int gxio_trio_trigger_host_interrupt_vf ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  mac,
unsigned int  can_spin,
unsigned long  msix_addr,
unsigned int  msix_data,
unsigned int  vf 
)

Trigger the PCIe RC host MSI/MSI-X interrupt for a virtual function.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
mac MAC number.
can_spin Flag indicating if this can spin.
msix_addr MSI-X message address.
msix_data MSI-X message data.
vf Virtual function instance number.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_ERR_BUSY.

int gxio_trio_write_mmi_bits ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  map,
unsigned int  bits,
gxio_trio_mmi_write_effect_t  mode 
)

Write the bus-to-tile interrupt state bits associated with a memory map region.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
map A Memory map region allocated by gxio_trio_alloc_memory_maps().
bits Bits to be written.
mode Select different memory map interrupt registers as the write target, which can generate different behavious.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_MEMORY_MAP or GXIO_ERR_INVAL.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c.

int gxio_trio_alloc_memory_maps ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  count,
unsigned int  first,
unsigned int  flags 
)

Allocate a memory map region for mapping incoming bus transactions to Tile memory loads and stores.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
count Number of memory map regions required.
first Index of first map region if GXIO_TRIO_ALLOC_FIXED flag is set, otherwise ignored.
flags Flag bits, including bits from gxio_trio_alloc_flags_e.
Returns:
Index of first memory map region, or GXIO_TRIO_ERR_NO_MEMORY_MAP if allocation failed.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and trio/push_dma/app.c.

int gxio_trio_enable_mmi ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
int  bind_cpu,
int  bind_event,
unsigned int  map,
gxio_trio_mmi_mode_t  mode 
)

Enable bus-to-tile interrupts for a particular memory map region.

Once enabled, the interrupt is masked until gxio_trio_unmask_mmi() is called.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
bind_cpu CPU number to which interrupt will be delivered.
bind_event Sub-interrupt event bit number.
map A memory map region allocated by gxio_trio_alloc_memory_maps().
mode Conditions that trigger the interrupt.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_MEMORY_MAP or GXIO_ERR_INTERRUPT or GXIO_ERR_INVAL.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c.

int gxio_trio_mask_mmi ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  map,
unsigned int  mask 
)

Prevent a bus-to-tile memory map interrupt from being delivered.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
map A memory map region allocated by gxio_trio_alloc_memory_maps().
mask Mask bits to be set to mask the memory map region interrupt.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_MEMORY_MAP or GXIO_ERR_INVAL.

int gxio_trio_unmask_mmi ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  map,
unsigned int  mask 
)

Allow a bus-to-tile memory map interrupt to be delivered.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
map A memory map region allocated by gxio_trio_alloc_memory_maps().
mask Mask bits to be set to unmask the memory map region interrupt.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_MEMORY_MAP or GXIO_ERR_INVAL.

int gxio_trio_read_mmi_bits ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  map 
)

Read the bus-to-tile interrupt state bits associated with a memory map region.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
map A Memory map region allocated by gxio_trio_alloc_memory_maps().
Returns:
16-bit value in case of a memory, otherwise GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_MEMORY_MAP.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c.

int gxio_trio_alloc_scatter_queues ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  count,
unsigned int  first,
unsigned int  flags 
)

Allocate scatter queues.

A scatter queue creates a window of bus address space that maps to memory addresses pulled out of a queue. Bus writes to the last word of the mapping region pops the queue and causes the next Tile-side buffer to be mapped in. Tile-side software can push new buffers onto the queue via gxio_trio_push_scatter_queue_buffer().

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
count Number of scatter queues required.
first Index of first scatter queue if GXIO_TRIO_ALLOC_FIXED flag is set, otherwise ignored.
flags Flag bits, including bits from gxio_trio_alloc_flags_e.
Returns:
Index of first scatter queue, or GXIO_TRIO_ERR_NO_SCATTER_QUEUE if allocation failed.

int gxio_trio_init_scatter_queue ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue,
uint64_t  size,
unsigned int  asid,
unsigned int  mac,
uint64_t  bus_address,
gxio_trio_order_mode_t  order_mode 
)

Initialize a scatter queue.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
queue A scatter queue returned by gxio_trio_alloc_scatter_queues().
size Size of the mapping region.
asid ASID used to translate Tile-side addresses.
mac MAC number.
bus_address Bus address at which the mapping starts.
order_mode Memory ordering mode for this mapping.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BUS_RANGE or GXIO_TRIO_ERR_INVAL.

void gxio_trio_push_scatter_queue_buffer ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue,
void *  buffer,
int  request_interrupt 
)

Push a buffer onto a scatter queue.

Software is responsible for guaranteeing that no more than 64 buffers are on the queue at a time.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
queue An initialized scatter queue.
buffer A 4kB aligned buffer in registered memory.
request_interrupt Pass 1 to request an interrupt when dequeued.

uint8_t gxio_trio_read_scatter_queue_pop_count ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  queue 
)

Find out how many scatter queue VAs have been dequeued.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
queue An initialized scatter queue.
Returns:
A 8-bit running count of how many scatter queue VAs (pushed by gxio_trio_push_scatter_queue_buffer()) have been popped.

int gxio_trio_enable_sqi ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
int  bind_cpu,
int  bind_event,
unsigned int  queue 
)

Enable bus-to-tile interrupts for a particular scatter queue.

NOTE that we only support user-space and level mode interrupt now.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
bind_cpu CPU number to which interrupt will be delivered.
bind_event Sub-interrupt event bit number.
queue An initialized scatter queue.
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_ERR_BAD_SCATTER_QUEUE or GXIO_ERR_INVAL.

int gxio_trio_alloc_pio_regions ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  count,
unsigned int  first,
unsigned int  flags 
)

Allocate programmed I/O (PIO) regions.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
count Number of PIO regions required.
first Index of first PIO region if GXIO_TRIO_ALLOC_FIXED flag is set, otherwise ignored.
flags Flag bits, including bits from gxio_trio_alloc_flags_e.
Returns:
Index of first PIO region, or GXIO_TRIO_ERR_NO_PIO if allocation failed.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and trio/push_dma/app.c.

int gxio_trio_free_pio_region ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  pio_region 
)

Free a PIO region.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
pio_region An initialized PIO region number.
Returns:
0 on success, GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_PIO on failure.

int gxio_trio_init_pio_region ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  pio_region,
unsigned int  mac,
uint64_t  bus_address,
unsigned int  flags 
)

Initialize a PIO region.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
pio_region A PIO region number returned by gxio_trio_alloc_pio_regions().
mac MAC number.
bus_address Start of bus address range to be mapped into virtual address space. Must be page aligned.
flags Flags including GXIO_TRIO_PIO_FLAG_ORDERED, GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_TRAFFIC_CLASS(), or GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_VFUNC().
Returns:
0 on success, GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_PIO or ENOMEM on failure.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and trio/push_dma/app.c.

int gxio_trio_unmap_pio_region ( void *  pio_mmio_addr,
unsigned int  length 
)

Unmap a PIO region from VA space.

Parameters:
pio_mmio_addr A PIO region's MMIO address returned by gxio_trio_map_pio_region().
length Size of the mappings.
Returns:
0 on success, or a negative error code.

void* gxio_trio_map_pio_region ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  pio_region,
unsigned int  length,
unsigned int  offset 
)

Map some or all of a PIO region into VA space.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
pio_region A PIO region number returned by gxio_trio_alloc_pio_regions() and initialized by gxio_trio_init_pio_region().
length Size of the mappings.
offset Offset within the PIO region.
Returns:
The PIO region's MMIO address, or MAP_FAILED (that is, (void *) -1) if not mapped.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and trio/push_dma/app.c.

void gxio_trio_write_uint64 ( void *  addr,
uint64_t  val 
)

Store eight bytes to a PIO mapping.

Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c.

void gxio_trio_write_uint32 ( void *  addr,
uint32_t  val 
)

Store four bytes to a PIO mapping.

void gxio_trio_write_uint16 ( void *  addr,
uint16_t  val 
)

Store two bytes to a PIO mapping.

void gxio_trio_write_uint8 ( void *  addr,
uint8_t  val 
)

Store a byte to a PIO mapping.

uint64_t gxio_trio_read_uint64 ( void *  addr  ) 

Load eight bytes from a PIO mapping.

Examples:
trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and trio/push_dma/app.c.

uint32_t gxio_trio_read_uint32 ( void *  addr  ) 

Load four bytes from a PIO mapping.

uint16_t gxio_trio_read_uint16 ( void *  addr  ) 

Load two bytes from a PIO mapping.

uint8_t gxio_trio_read_uint8 ( void *  addr  ) 

Load a byte from a PIO mapping.

int gxio_trio_alloc_push_dma_ring ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  count,
unsigned int  first,
unsigned int  flags 
)

Allocate push DMA rings.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
count Number of push DMA rings required.
first Index of first push DMA ring if GXIO_TRIO_ALLOC_FIXED flag is set, otherwise ignored.
flags Flag bits, including bits from gxio_trio_alloc_flags_e.
Returns:
Index of first push DMA ring, or GXIO_TRIO_ERR_NO_PUSH_DMA_RING if allocation failed.
Examples:
trio/push_dma/app.c.

int gxio_trio_alloc_pull_dma_ring ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  count,
unsigned int  first,
unsigned int  flags 
)

Allocate pull DMA rings.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
count Number of pull DMA rings required.
first Index of first pull DMA ring if GXIO_TRIO_ALLOC_FIXED flag is set, otherwise ignored.
flags Flag bits, including bits from gxio_trio_alloc_flags_e.
Returns:
Index of first pull DMA ring, or GXIO_TRIO_ERR_NO_PULL_DMA_RING if allocation failed.

int gxio_trio_init_push_dma_ring ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  ring,
unsigned int  mac,
unsigned int  asid,
unsigned int  req_flags,
void *  mem,
size_t  mem_size,
unsigned int  mem_flags 
)

Initialize a push DMA ring.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
ring Push DMA ring index.
mac MAC number.
asid ASID to be used for Tile-side address translation.
req_flags Flag bits, possibly including GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_VFUNC() or GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_TRAFFIC_CLASS().
mem A physically contiguous region of memory to be filled with a ring of gxio_trio_dma_desc_t structures.
mem_size Number of bytes in the ring. Must be 512, 2048, 8192 or 65536 * sizeof(gxio_trio_dma_desc_t).
mem_flags gxio_trio_mem_flags_e memory flags.
Returns:
0 on success, GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_PUSH_DMA_RING or GXIO_ERR_INVAL_MEMORY_SIZE on failure.

int gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_ring ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
unsigned int  ring,
unsigned int  mac,
unsigned int  asid,
unsigned int  req_flags,
void *  mem,
size_t  mem_size,
unsigned int  mem_flags 
)

Initialize a pull DMA ring.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
ring Pull DMA ring index.
mac MAC number.
asid ASID to be used for Tile-side address translation.
req_flags Flag bits, possibly including GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_VFUNC() or GXIO_TRIO_FLAG_TRAFFIC_CLASS().
mem A physically contiguous region of memory to be filled with a ring of gxio_trio_dma_desc_t structures.
mem_size Number of bytes in the ring. Must be 512, 2048, 8192 or 65536 * sizeof(gxio_trio_dma_desc_t).
mem_flags gxio_trio_mem_flags_e memory flags.
Returns:
0 on success, GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_PULL_DMA_RING or GXIO_ERR_INVAL_MEMORY_SIZE on failure.

int gxio_trio_enable_push_dma_isr ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
int  bind_cpu,
int  bind_event,
unsigned int  ring 
)

Enable push dma completion interrupt for a particular push dma ring.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
bind_cpu CPU number to which interrupt will be delivered.
bind_event Sub-interrupt event bit number.
ring A push dma ring allocated by gxio_trio_alloc_push_dma_ring().
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_PUSH_DMA_RING or GXIO_ERR_INTERRUPT or GXIO_ERR_INVAL.

int gxio_trio_enable_pull_dma_isr ( gxio_trio_context_t *  context,
int  bind_cpu,
int  bind_event,
unsigned int  ring 
)

Enable pull dma completion interrupt for a particular pull dma ring.

Parameters:
context An initialized TRIO context.
bind_cpu CPU number to which interrupt will be delivered.
bind_event Sub-interrupt event bit number.
ring A pull dma ring allocated by gxio_trio_alloc_pull_dma_ring().
Returns:
Zero on success, else GXIO_TRIO_ERR_BAD_PULL_DMA_RING or GXIO_ERR_INTERRUPT or GXIO_ERR_INVAL.

int64_t gxio_trio_dma_queue_try_reserve ( gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *  queue,
unsigned int  num 
)

Reserve slots for DMA commands, if possible.

Use gxio_trio_dma_queue_put_at() to actually populate the slots.

Parameters:
queue An dma queue initialized via gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue() or gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue().
num Number of slots to reserve.
Returns:
The first reserved slot, or a negative error code.

int gxio_trio_dma_queue_put ( gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *  queue,
gxio_trio_dma_desc_t  dma_desc 
)

Post a single DMA command to an DMA queue.

Parameters:
queue An dma queue initialized via gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue() or gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue().
dma_desc DMA command to be posted.
Returns:
0 on success.

bool gxio_trio_dma_queue_is_complete ( gxio_trio_dma_queue_t *  queue,
int64_t  slot,
bool  update 
)

Determine if a given DMA command has been completed.

Parameters:
queue An dma queue initialized via gxio_trio_init_push_dma_queue() or gxio_trio_init_pull_dma_queue().
slot The slot used by the DMA command.
update If true, and the command does not appear to have completed yet, then update any software cache of the hardware completion counter, and check again. This should normally be true.
Returns:
True iff the given DMA command has been completed.
Examples:
trio/push_dma/app.c.

All other trademarks and/or registered trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

Third-party software: The Tilera IDE makes use of the BeanShell scripting library. Source code for the BeanShell library can be found at the BeanShell website (http://www.beanshell.org/developer.html).

The following is a trademark of Marvell Semiconductor, Inc.: Distributed Switching Architecture (DSA).

THE INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THIS DOCUMENT IS PROVIDED ON AN "AS IS" BASIS. Tilera assumes no liability for damages arising directly or indirectly from any use of the information contained in this document.
